PaymentRequest.title: Payment Request
PaymentRequest.description: Records details of manual payment requests sent by potential customers. Can be approved manually by an admin or verified automatically.

fullName.description: The full name of the customer.

email.description: The email address of the customer.

whatsapp.description: The WhatsApp number of the customer.

plan.description: The subscription plan requested by the customer.

amount.description: The amount paid by the customer.

transactionId.description: The unique transaction ID from the payment provider.

screenshotBase64.description: Base64 encoded string of the payment screenshot uploaded by the customer.

status.description: The current status of the payment request.

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the payment request was created.

generatedUsername.description: The generated username for the user upon manual approval.

approvedAt.description: Timestamp when the request was manually approved.

verifiedAt.description: Timestamp when the payment was automatically verified.

userId.description: The UID of the user whose account was provisioned from this payment.

User.title: User
User.description: Represents an authenticated user of the application, storing profile information and system-specific attributes. This entity is used for all user types, including subscribers, creators, and administrators (admin staff), distinguished by the 'role' attribute.

uid.description: Unique user ID from Firebase Authentication, matching the document ID.

role.description: The authorization role of the user within the application.

disabled.description: Indicates if the user's account is disabled. `false` means active, `true` means disabled.

createdBy.description: The UID of the admin user who created or approved this account (optional).

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the user's profile was first created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when the user's profile was last updated.

subscriptionStart.description: Timestamp when the user's subscription starts (optional).

subscriptionEnd.description: Timestamp when the user's subscription expires (optional).

profileImageUrl.description: URL to the user's profile picture (optional).

phone.description: The user's phone number (optional, for super_admin).

permissions.description: Granular feature permissions for the user.

voiceClone.description: Access to the Voice Clone feature.

cinematic.description: Access to the Cinematic Camera feature.

wallet.description: Stores the user's credit balance for usage-based billing.

credits.description: The current number of available credits.

spent.description: The total number of credits spent by the user.

linkedPaymentRequestId.description: The ID of the payment request that led to the creation of this manual account (optional).

ManualAccount.title: Manual Account Log
ManualAccount.description: A log entry for a user account created manually by an admin from a payment request.

email.description: The email address of the created user.

userId.description: The generated username (e.g., guest_2024_001) for the user.

role.description: The role assigned to the user.

plan.description: The subscription plan assigned to the user.

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the log entry was created.

expiresAt.description: Timestamp when the user's subscription expires.

paymentRequestId.description: The ID of the payment request that initiated this manual account creation.

Project.title: Project
Project.description: Represents a top-level animation or creative project, containing scenes, assets, and other related data.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Project entity.

description.description: A detailed description of the project.

creationDate.description: Timestamp when the project was created.

lastModifiedDate.description: Timestamp when the project was last modified.

status.description: The current status of the project, e.g., active or archived.

timeline.description: The editing timeline for the project, composed of multiple tracks.

StoryIdea.title: StoryIdea
StoryIdea.description: Stores AI-generated story concepts, plotlines, character ideas, or scene outlines for animation projects.

id.description: Unique identifier for the StoryIdea entity.

title.description: A short, descriptive title for the story idea.

plotline.description: The generated main plot summary or synopsis.

characterConceptsJson.description: A JSON string containing detailed character concepts (e.g., names, personalities, designs, relationships).

sceneOutlinesJson.description: A JSON string containing outlines or descriptions of suggested scenes.

generationTimestamp.description: Timestamp when the story idea was generated.

generatedByUserId.description: Reference to User who generated this idea. (Relationship: User 1:N StoryIdea)

AssetCategory.title: AssetCategory
AssetCategory.description: Defines categories for organizing 2D assets within the library.

id.description: Unique identifier for the AssetCategory entity.

name.description: The name of the asset category (e.g., 'Fantasy Characters', 'Sci-Fi Backgrounds').

userId.description: Reference to User who owns this category. Allows for user-defined categories. (Relationship: User 1:N AssetCategory)

Asset.title: Asset
Asset.description: Represents an individual 2D media file, such as a character, prop, background image, or audio clip.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Asset entity.

name.description: The display name of the asset.

type.description: The type of the asset, categorizing its use.

fileUrl.description: URL or path to the actual asset file (e.g., image, audio file).

thumbnailUrl.description: URL or path to a smaller preview image (thumbnail) of the asset.

description.description: A detailed description of the asset.

tags.description: A list of keywords or tags for searching and filtering assets.

uploadedByUserId.description: Reference to User who uploaded this asset. (Relationship: User 1:N Asset)

uploadTimestamp.description: Timestamp when the asset was uploaded.

projectId.description: Optional reference to a Project, indicating if this asset is specific to a particular project rather than a general library asset. (Relationship: Project 1:N Asset)

usedInTimeline.description: Flag indicating if the asset is currently used in a timeline.

usedInExport.description: Flag indicating if the asset was part of a recent export.

Scene.title: Scene
Scene.description: Represents a static visual composition within a project, including a background and placed assets.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Scene entity.

name.description: The name of the scene (e.g., 'Opening Forest', 'City Chase').

projectId.description: Reference to Project this scene belongs to. (Relationship: Project 1:N Scene)

description.description: A description of the scene's content or purpose.

backgroundAssetId.description: Reference to the Asset used as the background for this scene. (Relationship: Asset 1:N Scene)

thumbnailUrl.description: URL or path to a thumbnail image representing the scene's visual content.

orderIndex.description: The sequential order of the scene within its project.

SceneAsset.title: SceneAsset
SceneAsset.description: Represents an instance of an Asset placed within a Scene, including its position, scale, rotation, and layering.

id.description: Unique identifier for the SceneAsset entity.

sceneId.description: Reference to Scene where this asset is placed. (Relationship: Scene 1:N SceneAsset)

assetId.description: Reference to the original Asset definition. (Relationship: Asset 1:N SceneAsset)

xPosition.description: The X-coordinate of the asset's position within the scene.

yPosition.description: The Y-coordinate of the asset's position within the scene.

scaleX.description: The horizontal scale factor of the asset (e.g., 1.0 for original size).

scaleY.description: The vertical scale factor of the asset (e.g., 1.0 for original size).

rotation.description: The rotation angle of the asset in degrees.

zIndex.description: The layering order of the asset within the scene (higher values appear on top).

propertiesJson.description: A JSON string for additional custom properties specific to this instance of the asset (e.g., color tint, flip status, opacity).

Timeline.title: Project Timeline
Timeline.description: Represents the editing timeline for a project, composed of multiple tracks containing asset references.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Timeline entity, typically the same as the project ID.

tracks.description: An array of tracks (video, audio, etc.) that make up the timeline.

version.description: A version number that increments on each save, used for snapshotting exports.

TimelineTrack.title: Timeline Track
TimelineTrack.description: A single track within a timeline, holding multiple asset references.

TimelineAsset.title: Timeline Asset Reference
TimelineAsset.description: A reference to an asset placed on a timeline track. It does not contain the asset data itself.

id.description: A unique ID for this specific instance of the asset on the timeline.

assetId.description: The ID of the source asset (e.g., from Asset collection).

start.description: Start time in seconds from the beginning of the timeline.

Keyframe.title: Keyframe
Keyframe.description: Stores a single data point for an animated property of a SceneAsset at a specific time within a Timeline.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Keyframe entity.

timelineId.description: Reference to Timeline this keyframe belongs to. (Relationship: Timeline 1:N Keyframe)

sceneAssetId.description: Reference to SceneAsset being animated by this keyframe. (Relationship: SceneAsset 1:N Keyframe)

timeMs.description: The time in milliseconds from the start of the timeline where this keyframe is set.

property.description: The name of the property being animated (e.g., 'xPosition', 'rotation', 'scaleX', 'opacity').

value.description: The value of the animated property at this keyframe. Stored as a string for flexibility across different property types (numbers, booleans, simple strings).

interpolationType.description: The type of interpolation used to transition to this keyframe from the previous one.

AudioTrack.title: AudioTrack
AudioTrack.description: Stores details about an audio segment (dialogue, music, sound effect) used within a scene or timeline.

id.description: Unique identifier for the AudioTrack entity.

name.description: The name of the audio track (e.g., 'Character A Dialogue', 'Background Music Loop').

fileUrl.description: URL or path to the audio file.

sceneId.description: Reference to Scene this audio track is associated with. (Relationship: Scene 1:N AudioTrack)

startTimeMs.description: The start time of the audio track relative to the beginning of the scene/timeline in milliseconds.

durationMs.description: The duration of the audio track in milliseconds.

volume.description: The volume level of the audio track (0.0 to 1.0).

addedByUserId.description: Reference to User who added or dubbed this audio track. (Relationship: User 1:N AudioTrack)

addedTimestamp.description: Timestamp when the audio track was added.

Watermark.title: Watermark
Watermark.description: Defines custom watermark images and their properties that users can apply to exported videos.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Watermark entity.

name.description: The name of the watermark (e.g., 'My Studio Logo', 'Draft Watermark').

imageUrl.description: URL or path to the watermark image file.

positionJson.description: A JSON string specifying the watermark's position (e.g., `{"preset": "bottom-right"}` or `{"x": 0.9, "y": 0.1}` for custom coordinates).

opacity.description: The opacity level of the watermark (0.0 for fully transparent, 1.0 for fully opaque).

userId.description: Reference to User who owns this watermark. (Relationship: User 1:N Watermark)

isDefault.description: Indicates if this watermark is the user's default choice for new exports.

StylizedConversion.title: StylizedConversion
StylizedConversion.description: Records requests and results for the AI-powered cartoon stylization feature.

id.description: Unique identifier for the StylizedConversion entity.

sourceFileUrl.description: URL to the original image or video file submitted for conversion.

outputFileUrl.description: URL to the cartoon-stylized output file.

conversionSettingsJson.description: A JSON string detailing the specific style applied and other conversion parameters (e.g., 'anime', 'comic book', 'watercolor' style names, intensity).

status.description: The current status of the conversion job.

requestedByUserId.description: Reference to User who requested this conversion. (Relationship: User 1:N StylizedConversion)

requestTimestamp.description: Timestamp when the conversion was requested.

completionTimestamp.description: Timestamp when the conversion was completed.

projectId.description: Optional reference to Project if this conversion is part of a specific project. (Relationship: Project 1:N StylizedConversion)

ExportJob.title: ExportJob
ExportJob.description: Records details about a video or image sequence export operation from a project.

id.description: Unique identifier for the ExportJob entity.

projectName.description: The name of the project being exported.

format.description: The output format of the export.

resolution.description: The target resolution for the export.

status.description: The current status of the export job.

priority.description: The priority of the export job in the queue.

outputFileUrl.description: URL to the final exported file.

requestedByUserId.description: Reference to User who initiated the export.

requestTimestamp.description: Timestamp when the export was requested.

completionTimestamp.description: Timestamp when the export was completed.

timelineVersion.description: A version number of the timeline state that was exported.

assetVersion.description: A version number of the assets used in the export.

failureReason.description: Reason for export failure, e.g., 'Missing linked asset'.

LiveStreamConfiguration.title: LiveStreamConfiguration
LiveStreamConfiguration.description: Stores user-defined settings and preferences for live streaming sessions.

id.description: Unique identifier for the LiveStreamConfiguration entity.

userId.description: Reference to User who owns this configuration. (Relationship: User 1:N LiveStreamConfiguration)

name.description: A descriptive name for the streaming configuration (e.g., 'Gaming Setup', 'Tutorial Stream Settings').

rtmpUrl.description: The RTMP server URL for streaming (e.g., rtmp://a.rtmp.youtube.com/live2).

streamKey.description: The unique stream key required for authentication with the RTMP server. Note: Sensitive data, implement secure storage/retrieval.

platform.description: The target streaming platform or 'Custom RTMP'.

resolution.description: The video resolution for the live stream.

bitrateKbps.description: The target video bitrate in kilobits per second.

cameraMode.description: The aspect ratio or capture mode for the camera.

enableAudioEchoReduction.description: Boolean indicating if audio echo reduction should be enabled.

enableAudioNoiseReduction.description: Boolean indicating if audio noise reduction should be enabled.

isDefault.description: Indicates if this is the user's default live stream configuration.

LiveStreamFilter.title: LiveStreamFilter
LiveStreamFilter.description: Defines OpenGL ES visual effects (filters) that can be applied to a live stream.

id.description: Unique identifier for the LiveStreamFilter entity.

name.description: The display name of the filter (e.g., 'Cinematic LUT A', 'Sepia Tone', 'Gaussian Blur').

type.description: The general category or type of the filter.

parametersJson.description: A JSON string containing filter-specific parameters (e.g., blur radius, color matrix, LUT path, intensity).

shaderCode.description: Optional: Raw GLSL shader code for custom filters, or a reference to a built-in shader asset.

thumbnailUrl.description: URL to a preview image demonstrating the filter's effect.

userId.description: Optional reference to User who created this custom filter. (Relationship: User 1:N LiveStreamFilter)

LiveStreamConfigurationFilter.title: LiveStreamConfigurationFilter
LiveStreamConfigurationFilter.description: Junction entity to manage the many-to-many relationship between LiveStreamConfiguration and LiveStreamFilter, including the order of application.

id.description: Unique identifier for the LiveStreamConfigurationFilter entity.

orderIndex.description: The sequential order in which this filter is applied within the configuration's FX pipeline.

LiveStreamSession.title: LiveStreamSession
LiveStreamSession.description: Records historical data for past live streaming sessions, including status, duration, and associated configuration.

id.description: Unique identifier for the LiveStreamSession entity.

userId.description: Reference to User who conducted the live stream. (Relationship: User 1:N LiveStreamSession)

liveStreamConfigurationId.description: Reference to LiveStreamConfiguration used for this session. (Relationship: LiveStreamConfiguration 1:N LiveStreamSession)

title.description: The title of the live stream as displayed to viewers.

description.description: A detailed description of the live stream's content.

startTime.description: Timestamp when the live stream officially started.

endTime.description: Optional timestamp when the live stream officially ended. Null if still ongoing or interrupted.

durationSeconds.description: The total duration of the live stream in seconds.

platform.description: The platform where the stream was broadcasted.

viewershipCount.description: The peak or final viewership count for the session.

recordingUrl.description: Optional URL to the recorded version of the live stream if saved by the platform.

status.description: The current or final status of the live stream session.

AdminNotification.title: Admin Notification
AdminNotification.description: Records events that require admin attention, such as new payment requests.

type.description: The type of event being notified.

referenceId.description: The ID of the document related to the notification (e.g., payment request ID).

message.description: A short message describing the event.

isRead.description: Indicates if an admin has viewed the notification.

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the notification was created.

SubscriptionPlan.title: Subscription Plan
SubscriptionPlan.description: Defines the available subscription plans, including their name, duration, and price.

id.description: Unique identifier for the subscription plan.

name.description: The name of the plan, used as an identifier.

durationInMonths.description: The duration of the subscription plan in months.

price.description: The price of the subscription plan.

isActive.description: Indicates if the plan is currently available for purchase.

QrSetting.title: QR Code Setting
QrSetting.description: Stores details about the active QR code for manual payments.

qrImage.description: The URL of the active QR code image.

active.description: Indicates if the QR code is currently active and should be displayed to users.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when the QR code setting was last updated.

definition.description: Stores public profile information for all users. The document ID matches the user's Firebase Auth UID. User-specific data is stored here.

Root collection for all projects. Each project document contains an 'ownerId' field to link it to a user in the '/users' collection. Project documents also contain arrays of sub-documents for characters, scenes, etc.
Stores details of manual payment requests sent by guests. Readable/manageable only by admins.
Stores the active QR code settings for payments. Writable only by admins.
Defines available subscription plans. Readable by authenticated users, writable by admins.
Records administrative actions performed within the app. Readable/writable only by admins.
Logs critical system errors and events. Writable by the system, readable by admins.
Stores support tickets submitted by users. Writable by authenticated users, readable/manageable by admins.
Stores a log of manually created user accounts for administrative tracking.
Stores notifications for admins. Writable by authenticated users, readable/updatable by admins.

firestore.reasoning: The Firestore structure uses a root collection model for primary entities like `users` and `projects`. This design simplifies queries, especially for administrators who need to view all projects regardless of the owner. Security is enforced via rules that check for an `ownerId` field within each project document, aligning with the Query-as-you-Pay (QAP) principle by avoiding cross-document reads in list queries. Other system-level collections (`paymentRequests`, `qr_settings`, etc.) are also at the root and are secured for admin-only access. This flat structure, combined with document-level ownership fields, provides a scalable and secure data model.
